capabilities: Ensure data consistency models are evaluated with precise metrics and validation steps, integrating agent planning derived knowledge bases.

procedure.action: Capture the specification of the data consistency model using domain-specific tools like Apache Kafka or RabbitMQ; ensure the model aligns with the agent planning derived knowledge base using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Assert that the error count is less than 1 and the result is valid by running automated tests using tools like Postman or JUnit; log operation duration and result code to an audit trail using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Log operation duration in milliseconds and result code to an audit trail using structured logging frameworks like ELK Stack or Splunk for traceability and monitoring using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Assert the specification against the domain's requirements using tools like JSON Schema or XML Schema validation; ensure all fields conform to expected formats and constraints by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Inject criteria and metrics for evaluation using domain-appropriate tooling such as Prometheus or Grafana; ensure metrics are collected and visualized for ongoing assessment by injecting the component into the dependency graph; assert inject_success = true and circular_dependency_count < 1; log injection_target and injection_time_ms.

Assert the procedure output by running integration tests with tools like Selenium or Cucumber; assert that error count remains below 1 and results are consistently valid by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

summary: Establish a framework for evaluating data consistency models in distributed systems using agent planning derived knowledge bases, with clear metrics and validation steps.
title: Data Consistency Model Evaluation in Distributed Systems

failureConditions: Inconsistent data when the specification omits validation steps, causing downstream systems to receive malformed data.
High error rates when automated tests are not comprehensive, leading to undetected failures in data consistency models.
Loss of traceability when operation logs are incomplete, causing difficulty in diagnosing issues in distributed systems.
metrics: that the error count is less than 1 and the result is valid by running automated tests using tools like Postman or JUnit
the specification against the domain's requirements using tools like JSON Schema or XML Schema validation
the procedure output by running integration tests with tools like Selenium or Cucumber
that error count remains below 1 and results are consistently valid by asserting all required fields conform to the sche
